name: AI Security Guard
description: Real-time AI agent input/output security scanning platform. Detects and blocks prompt injection, data exfiltration, jailbreaking, and adversarial inputs before they reach LLMs. Sits as middleware between users/agents and the AI model, acting as a firewall for agentic systems.
oneLiner: Real-time AI agent security scanning with prompt injection detection, data leak prevention, and policy enforcement.
pricing: Usage-based per scan. Free tier for evaluation. Enterprise custom pricing.
capabilities: SDK for LLM providers and agent frameworks

uxAnalysis.builderFlow: Not agent builder. Integrate SDK, configure policies via web console: select threat categories, set thresholds, define custom patterns.
strengths: Addresses critical gap in enterprise agent security
weaknesses: False positive/negative rates are industry challenge
notableFeatures: Prompt injection detection via specialized ML
Multi-turn jailbreak detection across conversation context

recentDevelopments: 2026-04: Microsoft Agent Governance Toolkit — open-source, addresses all 10 OWASP agentic AI risks with sub-ms policy enforcement
2026-03: Cisco Reimagines Security for Agentic Workforce — Detection Studio and Malware Threat Reversing Agent GA
2026-03: Tencent AI-Infra-Guard expands to 55 AI components, launches EdgeOne ClawScan + Agent-Scan
2026: Salt Agentic Security Platform — first dedicated solution for AI agent-to-enterprise data security
2026: 78.6% of security leaders report increased executive scrutiny of AI risks, only 23.5% find legacy tools effective
SDK integrations for LangChain, CrewAI, OpenAI/Anthropic
Growing partnerships with agent platform vendors

deploymentOptions.notes: Enterprise security product with on-prem option for sensitive environments.

subCriteria.name: Agent Builder UX
subCriteria.rationale: Policy config via web console. Not agent builder.

subCriteria.name: Time-to-Value
subCriteria.rationale: Policy testing with samples. Scan result inspector.

subCriteria.name: Debugging & Testing
subCriteria.rationale: API docs and integration guides. Early-stage depth.

subCriteria.name: Documentation & Learning
subCriteria.rationale: Policy changes immediate. No staged rollout.

subCriteria.name: Multi-Agent Coordination
subCriteria.rationale: Not orchestration. Security scanning service.

subCriteria.name: Human-in-the-Loop Design
subCriteria.rationale: Flagged threat review as basic human interaction.

subCriteria.name: Workflow Templates
subCriteria.rationale: Single-team policy management.

subCriteria.name: Team Development
subCriteria.rationale: Some pre-built policy templates.

subCriteria.name: Native Connectors
subCriteria.rationale: SDKs for OpenAI, Anthropic, LangChain, CrewAI. Webhooks.

subCriteria.name: API & Protocol Standards
subCriteria.rationale: REST API. Python/JS SDKs. Early MCP. No gRPC.

subCriteria.name: Authentication & Access
subCriteria.rationale: API keys. SSO on enterprise. Basic.

subCriteria.name: Data Pipeline & RAG
subCriteria.rationale: No data pipeline. Operates on individual inputs.

subCriteria.name: Guardrails & Kill Switches
subCriteria.rationale: THIS IS the guardrails product. Real-time blocking. Policy enforcement.

subCriteria.name: Audit Trail & Compliance
subCriteria.rationale: Flagged inputs routed for review. Basic quarantine.

subCriteria.name: RBAC & Access Control
subCriteria.rationale: Scan logs comprehensive. Compliance certs in progress.

subCriteria.name: Explainability & Transparency
subCriteria.rationale: Basic admin/viewer roles. Team management in enterprise.

subCriteria.name: Deployment Flexibility
subCriteria.rationale: Cloud scanning service. SDK for inline. On-prem may be available.

subCriteria.name: Scaling & Monitoring
subCriteria.rationale: High-throughput, low-latency by design. Auto-scaling.

subCriteria.name: Cost Management
subCriteria.rationale: Threat dashboards, scan metrics, policy match rates.

subCriteria.name: Ecosystem Maturity
subCriteria.rationale: Early-stage. Small customer base. Growing AI security visibility.

scoringRationale: 55/100 overall. Strongest: Governance (14/20). Weakest: Orchestration (5/20). Breakdown: Creation (12/20), Orchestration (5/20), Integration (11/20), Governance (14/20), Operations (12/20).
targetUser: Security teams protecting AI agent deployments from attacks

walkthrough.title: Detecting and Blocking Prompt Injection Attacks

steps.title: Integrate the Security SDK
steps.description: Add AI Security Guard's lightweight SDK to your agent pipeline — it wraps your LLM calls with input/output scanning. One-line integration for LangChain, CrewAI, or custom agent frameworks. Under 5ms latency overhead.

works: Purpose-built for AI security — deeper than bolt-on guardrails in general platforms
Prompt injection detection is among the most comprehensive available
API-first design integrates into any agent pipeline
doesntWork: Security-only tool requires integration with a separate agent platform
Configuration requires security expertise — not accessible to general developers
Evaluation metrics for detection quality are not self-serve
pmCritique.userPsychology: Security teams trust specialized tools over general-platform guardrails — the depth of prompt injection detection signals serious engineering. But integration overhead means it only gets adopted when there is a dedicated security team championing it.

steps.title: Configure Threat Detection Policies
steps.description: Define what threats to monitor: prompt injection attempts, jailbreak patterns, PII leakage, toxic content, and off-topic manipulation. Set sensitivity thresholds and choose block vs. alert mode for each threat category.

works: Real-time threat detection with sub-100ms latency
Configurable sensitivity levels balance security vs usability
Detailed threat reports with classification and confidence scores
doesntWork: False positive tuning requires production traffic — cannot calibrate pre-deployment
No visual dashboard for security posture overview
Integration documentation assumes advanced security knowledge
pmCritique.userPsychology: Low latency is critical — security that slows down the user experience gets disabled. The sub-100ms performance means developers cannot use latency as an excuse to skip security.

steps.title: Real-Time Input Scanning
steps.description: Every user message is scanned before reaching your LLM. The detector identifies injection patterns — hidden instructions, role hijacking, delimiter attacks, and encoded payloads. Malicious inputs are blocked with an explanation.

steps.title: Output Monitoring and Data Loss Prevention
steps.description: Agent responses are scanned for accidental PII exposure, credential leaks, and unauthorized data disclosure. DLP rules prevent your agent from revealing internal system prompts, API keys, or customer data.

steps.title: Audit Trail and Incident Response
steps.description: Every blocked threat is logged with full context — the original input, detection confidence, and policy matched. Security teams get real-time alerts and can review incident timelines to tune detection rules.

pmVerdict.summary: AI Security Guard — limited public demo content available. Verdict based on documentation and existing research data only. Score reflects capability claims that could not be verified through video walkthrough.
strengths: See existing capability analysis in the product profile
risks: No video demo available for verification
Product capabilities based on documentation only
UX quality unverifiable without live demo

ctoVerdict.summary: Insufficient public demo material to evaluate AI Security Guard for enterprise adoption. Recommend requesting a live demo from the vendor before making any commitment.
strengths: See existing scoring for capability assessment
dealBreakers: Cannot verify UX claims without walkthrough

youtubeReviews.title: Securing AI Agents: How to Prevent Hidden Prompt Injection Attacks
Why Prompt Injection Guardrails Fail in Browser Agents

coreCompetence.bestAt: AI-native security guardrails — purpose-built to detect and prevent prompt injection, data leakage, and adversarial attacks on AI agent systems.
coreCompetence.targetUser: Security teams responsible for protecting AI deployments. Enterprises deploying agents in regulated environments who need compliance-grade protection.
coreCompetence.vsAlternatives: Wins on security specialization — deeper than bolt-on guardrails in general platforms. Wins on threat detection depth. Loses on breadth — security-only, not an agent builder. Must be integrated with a separate agent platform.
